hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent (MatAgent) is integrated with domain-specific tools (short- and long-term memory, periodic table, knowledge base), a diffusion-based crystal structure generator (DiffCSP), and a GNN-based property evaluator (iComFormer), then it will more frequently generate inorganic compositions and structures that meet specified formation-energy targets and achieve higher validity, uniqueness, and novelty metrics than tool-free LLM variants and diffusion-only baselines because the tools provide targeted knowledge, iterative feedback, and structure-property evaluation that guide and refine generation.
